posture: Quiet Sunday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, AI workforce readiness, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; Saturday's watchlist yield (Wollongong's Canva Goals world-first, the MAV/ALGA National AI Working Group and the three-council Adelaide AI partnership, all logged 18 July) is not repeated here.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a–§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Recurring or undated items were again reviewed and excluded: CleanBins' AI kerbside contamination-detection results (98%+ accuracy across six NSW/VIC councils) rest on trials reported March–May and were not the subject of a fresh dated development today; the NSW commercial FOGO source-separation mandate (in force from 1 July 2026) is a regulatory-rollout milestone rather than a new innovation signal; Inner West Council's public-place food/container recycling baskets ran May–June; and the UK's Heriot-Watt 'AI Readiness of UK Local Authorities' study, though in-scope by function, is prior-period research (already older than yesterday's logged Staffordshire item by outlet ordering) and out of window. Overseas and network material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026 Madrid readouts and the Mayors AI Forum, the Bloomberg Harvard City Leadership Initiative 2026 class, LGIU/LocalGov AI-in-planning coverage such as the MHCLG Extract rollout and the Rother/Valon and Greater Cambridge planning pilots) was either out-of-window, UK/US policy-network content already treated as background, or unchanged today. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title (AFR/SMH/LGC/MJ) was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Today is a Sunday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. Sections are published empty per §6.
